title: Lexical Issues: The Role of Whitespace in Java
description: A visual breakdown of how whitespace characters are treated by the Java compiler and their practical uses in code formatting.
elements: Whitespace Characters (space, tab, newline, form feed)
relationships: Java Source Code is read by the Compiler
Compiler lexically analyzes code into a Token Stream
Whitespace Characters are generally Ignored and discarded
Meaningful Whitespace inside String literals is preserved
